suggested_automation: Implement an automated appointment scheduling and reminder communication system, including self-service options for patients to check and change appointments, and reliable multi-channel reminder delivery with confirmation tracking.
reasoning: Automating routine appointment inquiries and reminders will reduce staff workload, improve patient experience, and reduce missed appointments caused by communication gaps.
request: Our front desk staff spends hours every day answering the same appointment questions over email and phone. Patients also say reminder emails occasionally aren't sent. We'd like a better way to handle scheduling and communication.

suggested_automation: Automate renaming, sorting, and filing of incoming PDF documents, and set up notification alerts for attorneys when new documents arrive.
reasoning: Automating document management reduces manual workload and speeds attorney notifications, improving workflow efficiency.
request: We receive hundreds of PDFs from clients every week. Someone has to rename each file, move it into the correct folder, and tell the attorneys that new documents have arrived.

suggested_automation: Implement a workflow automation system where housekeeping can directly log maintenance requests into a centralized platform with status tracking and notifications to managers.
reasoning: Automating and centralizing maintenance requests will reduce duplicates and forgotten issues while increasing visibility for managers. Priority and risk cannot be fully assessed without further info on impact.
request: Housekeeping currently texts maintenance when they find something broken. Requests are frequently forgotten or duplicated, and managers don't know what's been completed.
